=========================================================================
 OptimalFit - Android + iOS build info
=========================================================================
Build date      : 2026-07-09 (Phase 3 + polish build — the FINAL native
                  rebuild by Infra-4 on the macOS machine against the
                  FROZEN Phase-3 app/ web code PLUS the post-Phase-3 polish
                  fixes. This SUPERSEDES the Infra-3 Phase-3 build
                  [2026-07-08]. The native toolchain/recipe is unchanged;
                  the web snapshot and iOS Info.plist advanced, so the
                  artifact hashes below are new.)

*** WHAT CHANGED vs the Infra-3 (Phase-3) build — "Phase 3 + polish" ***
  1. iOS share fix: app/js/receipts.js now uses dataUrlToBlob() so the
     Receipt card shares correctly through the iOS share sheet.
  2. Service worker: app/sw.js bumped to VERSION "v13" (was v12 at
     Phase-3/Receipts). NOTE: www/sw.js remains the intentional
     self-unregistering no-op — the v13 version lives in the REAL app/sw.js
     (the PWA worker); never "fix" the native no-op.
  3. iOS camera/photo crash fix: native/ios/App/App/Info.plist gained the
     three usage strings NSCameraUsageDescription,
     NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription and NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription.
     Without these iOS terminates the app the moment it touches the camera
     or photo library (meal / physique / community photos, Receipt save).
     These strings are VERIFIED PRESENT IN THE BUILT PRODUCT (see below),
     not just the source plist.
  4. New iOS export option plists exist for a later signed export:
     native/ios/exportOptions.plist and exportOptions-development.plist.
  js file count: 31 (30 top-level app/js + js/vendor/supabase.js), mirrored
  byte-identically into both android assets/public/js and ios
  App/App/public/js (31 = 31 = 31). copy-web reports 39 total files copied.

*** KEYSTORE — PROVISIONAL (unchanged from Infra-2/Infra-3) ***
  A keystore generated on this Mac (2026-07-08, Infra-2) is reused:
    dist/optimalfit-release.keystore (PKCS12, alias optimalfit, RSA 2048,
    10000 days, CN=OptimalFit)
    cert SHA-256: 5bf1776f8d7cbcaa1aef15a459e6e772bbdd839a7d82674ca58bb64a7fe216a8
  Passwords + loss warning: see dist/SIGNING-INFO.txt.
  This keystore is PROVISIONAL — the user must either transfer the original
  Windows keystore or bless this new one (the app is not yet on Play, so
  either works). The VERIFIED deliverable is the debug APK; the signed
  release/AAB "work mechanically, pending the user's keystore decision".
  IF an AAB signed with the OLD Windows keystore (cert 7e4cb149...cfcd9e1)
  was EVER uploaded to Play, use THAT keystore — this new one is rejected.

App identity
  applicationId : com.optimalfit.app
  versionCode   : 1   (UNCHANGED — nothing has been uploaded to Play yet.
                       The user must bump versionCode +1 on their FIRST
                       Play upload, and +1 on every upload thereafter;
                       Play rejects reused versionCodes.)
  versionName   : 1.0.0
  label         : OptimalFit

SDK levels (from native/android/variables.gradle - Capacitor 8.4.1 template)
  minSdk 24 (Android 7.0) / targetSdk 36 / compileSdk 36

Toolchain (macOS, Apple Silicon) — unchanged from Infra-2/Infra-3
  JDK           : Homebrew OpenJDK (/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk). Capacitor 8
                  generates sourceCompatibility Java 21 in
                  capacitor.build.gradle; the Homebrew JDK compiles fine.
  Android SDK   : /Users/krishjetly/Library/Android/sdk
                  (cmdline-tools, platform-tools, platforms;android-36,
                  build-tools;36.0.0)
  Gradle        : 8.14.3 (wrapper) / AGP 8.13.0
  Xcode         : 26.6 (17F113); iPhone 17 simulator runtime installed.

Web snapshot built (webDir = native/www, mirrored from app/)
  Synced        : 2026-07-09 via `npm run sync` (Infra-4). copy-web mirrored
                  app/ -> native/www (39 files) and replaced sw.js with the
                  self-unregistering no-op; cap sync populated both
                  android assets/public and ios App/App/public.
  Content       : FROZEN Phase-3 app/ + polish (iOS share fix, sw v13).

Artifacts (SHA-256) — Phase 3 + polish build, rebuilt 2026-07-09 (Infra-4).
  Hashes differ from the Infra-3 build because the web snapshot advanced
  (receipts.js share fix, sw v13) + normal local build nondeterminism;
  signing key is the same.
  OptimalFit-debug.apk    4,333,689 B  8bfdf592bee979e2c302aa36bd519eceb0bb3288a16efc4c938bef585b7c268d
                          (debug-signed with this Mac's standard debug key —
                           VERIFIED deliverable)
  OptimalFit-release.apk  3,339,271 B  7e511c6c2180099ec8148b58e3dc8a2f74b9f22f935fc39e3412cf0e8789a174
                          (signed: CN=OptimalFit, cert SHA-256
                           5bf1776f8d7cbcaa1aef15a459e6e772bbdd839a7d82674ca58bb64a7fe216a8)
  OptimalFit-release.aab  3,178,232 B  60c64437e91d04a85d4a4e6919085dbdb82570ee2b8249b84593720e5568402d
                          (jarsigner: "jar verified" — upload THIS to Play,
                           after the user resolves the keystore + bumps
                           versionCode)

Verification performed (2026-07-09, Infra-4)
  - apksigner verify --print-certs on release APK: OK, cert SHA-256
    5bf1776f...16a8 matches the provisional keystore.
  - jarsigner -verify on AAB: "jar verified" (self-signed / no-timestamp
    warnings expected for a provisional key).
  - Polish byte-identity INSIDE the debug APK (unzip assets/public/js/
    receipts.js | diff vs app/js/receipts.js):
      js/receipts.js IDENTICAL — sha256
      17d16cf0a546d4e9eb97de324e8adeeb04d3df4ba7ba260782505f3ff84e2d27
      and contains the dataUrlToBlob() iOS share fix, confirming the polish
      web code is really bundled.
  - www/sw.js is the intentional no-op (unchanged, by design); the v13
    worker lives in app/sw.js.

iOS (Phase 3 + polish, all three levels — Infra-4, 2026-07-09)
  1. COMPILES: xcodebuild -project native/ios/App/App.xcodeproj -scheme App
       -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=<udid>' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
       build  ->  ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **
  2. RUNS: installed + launched com.optimalfit.app on the iPhone 17
     simulator (udid 5BFE0D39-9D1F-400E-8175-693BA6070BCB). App started and
     rendered the onboarding/welcome screen without crashing.
     Screenshot via `simctl io <udid> screenshot` (Infra-4 scratchpad,
     optimalfit-final-sim.png).
  3. CAMERA STRING VERIFIED IN BUILD: plutil -p on the BUILT sim
     App.app/Info.plist AND on the archived App.app/Info.plist both show all
     three usage strings present:
       NSCameraUsageDescription
       NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription
       NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription
     (Not merely in the source plist — confirmed in the built product, which
      is what fixes the camera/photo-library launch crash.)
  4. ARCHIVES: xcodebuild archive -project native/ios/App/App.xcodeproj
       -scheme App -configuration Release CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
       -archivePath <scratch>/OptimalFit-final.xcarchive
       -destination 'generic/platform=iOS'
     ->  ** ARCHIVE SUCCEEDED **  (UNSIGNED; signing/export needs an Apple
     Developer team — exportOptions.plist + cloud-CI route in
     docs/IOS-SHIP-GUIDE.md is the backup). The archived
     App.app/Info.plist carries all 3 usage strings.
     Archive path (this run, in the Infra-4 scratchpad):
       <scratchpad>/OptimalFit-final.xcarchive
  Capacitor 8 iOS uses Swift Package Manager (capacitor-swift-pm 8.4.1
  resolved from GitHub) — NO CocoaPods, no Podfile; do not install Pods.

Rebuild recipe — macOS (THIS machine) — VERIFIED WORKING 2026-07-09 by Infra-4
  One-time setup (already done on this Mac; only redo on a fresh machine):
    - Android cmdline-tools under ~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest
    - yes | sdkmanager --licenses
      yes | sdkmanager "platforms;android-36" "build-tools;36.0.0" "platform-tools"
    - ~/.gradle/gradle.properties containing:
        systemProp.javax.net.ssl.trustStoreType=pkcs12
      *** CRITICAL: the TRACKED native/android/gradle.properties sets
      trustStoreType=Windows-ROOT (for the old Windows box's TLS
      interception); on macOS that keystore type doesn't exist and ALL
      Gradle downloads fail with "problem accessing trust store". The
      user-home gradle.properties overrides it for BOTH the wrapper JVM
      and the daemon. GRADLE_OPTS / CLI -D do NOT stick for the wrapper.
      Infra-4 VERIFIED this file was still present; do NOT edit the tracked
      project file (would dirty git).
    - native/android/local.properties:  sdk.dir=/Users/krishjetly/Library/Android/sdk
    - dist/optimalfit-release.keystore + dist/keystore.properties
      (storeFile=../../../dist/optimalfit-release.keystore — resolved
      relative to native/android/app)
    - Xcode: xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch ; xcodebuild -downloadPlatform iOS
  Per release (after any app/ change):
    1. Bump versionCode (+1) and versionName in
       native/android/app/build.gradle BEFORE a Play upload (see above —
       still 1 because nothing is on Play yet).
    2. export JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk
       cd /Users/krishjetly/fitness_app/Fitness-Coach-app/native
       npm install        # first time only
       npm run sync
       cd android
       sh ./gradlew assembleDebug bundleRelease assembleRelease
       # "sh ./gradlew" — gradlew is committed WITHOUT the exec bit; plain
       # ./gradlew fails "permission denied". Do NOT chmod +x (dirties git).
    3. Artifacts under native/android/app/build/outputs/
       (apk/debug, apk/release, bundle/release) — copy to dist/ as
       OptimalFit-debug.apk / OptimalFit-release.apk / OptimalFit-release.aab
       and update the hashes here (shasum -a 256).
    Signing is automatic: app/build.gradle reads dist/keystore.properties.
    iOS: build for a booted sim (CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO), install+launch
    com.optimalfit.app, VERIFY the camera usage string is in the built
    App.app/Info.plist (plutil -p ... | grep -i camera), then archive to a
    *.xcarchive with -destination 'generic/platform=iOS'
    CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO.
=========================================================================
